CFO

The financial operating system.

CFO is a local-first financial operating system powered by Steward—not a budgeting toy, not a generic tracker. It interprets, analyzes, and guides financial decisions from structured data and clear rules.

$

A financial operating system—not a dashboard toy

CFO is strictly about money in motion: structure, planning, and decisions grounded in your data. It is not a generic finance tracker and not “just” budgeting software—it is the system that interprets, analyzes, and guides using rules and real inputs.

Steward inside CFO interprets financial data, detects patterns and risks, and calculates projections and runway. Guidance is deterministic: derived from what you have recorded, not invented.

What CFO is built to handle

  • Budgeting and financial planning
  • Forecasting and cash-flow understanding
  • OCR ingestion for statements and documents
  • Optional Plaid integration—deliberate, not a default
  • Structured financial intelligence and advanced organization

Local-first and privacy-first by design. Your books stay oriented to your machine and your choices.

Steward without guesswork

Steward is a controlled, rules-based, logic-driven layer—not vague “AI magic.” In CFO it produces projections and signals you can trace back to sources and rules.

No hallucinated balances, no filler numbers. Where data is missing, CFO does not pretend it is not.

Same ecosystem, different jobs

CFO is the flagship financial title. Eden is standalone research software; Ledger is a standalone identity vault and document-execution system. They are not modes inside CFO—they are separate products that share engineering discipline, visual language, and Steward as the common intelligence layer.

Eden Ledger Compare all

In development

Not available for purchase, download, or access. This is being built.

Currently being built
